subject: ap-precalculus
title: Sinusoidal Modeling: Ferris Wheel & Passenger Height
stimulus: A Ferris wheel has a diameter of $60$ meters and its center is $35$ meters above the ground. The wheel completes one full revolution every $4$ minutes. A passenger boards at the lowest point of the wheel at time $t = 0$ minutes. The sinusoidal function $H$ models the height of the passenger above the ground, in meters, as a function of time $t$, in minutes.

parts.prompt:
The function $H$ can be written in the form $H(t) = a\sin(b(t + c)) + d$.

(i) Find the values of constants $a$, $b$, $c$, and $d$.

(ii) Find the height of the passenger at $t = 1$ minute. Show the computations that lead to your answer.
parts.scoring_notes: Multiple valid parameterizations. Canonical: $H(t) = -30\sin(\frac{\pi}{2}(t+1)) + 35$ (starts at minimum). Also accept $H(t) = 30\sin(\frac{\pi}{2}(t-1)) + 35$. Key constraints: max $= 65$ m, min $= 5$ m, period $= 4$ min, $H(0) = 5$ (minimum). Verify: $-30\sin(\pi/2) + 35 = -30 + 35 = 5$ ✓. For part (ii), $H(1) = -30\sin(\pi) + 35 = 35$ m. If student uses an alternate valid form, full credit if substitution is correct.

scoring_points.description: Correct parameter values with $|a| = 30$, $d = 35$, $b = \frac{\pi}{2}$, and $c$ consistent with $H(0) = 5$ (minimum)

alternatives.correct_example: $a = -30$ (amplitude $= 30$, negative for minimum start), $d = 35$ (midline = center height), $b = \pi/2$ (period $= 4$ min), $c = 1$ (phase: $H(0) = -30\sin(\pi/2) + 35 = 5$ ✓)

wrong_examples: $d = 0$ (forgetting the vertical shift — center is 35 m above ground)
common_traps: Since the passenger starts at the lowest point, the function must give its minimum at $t = 0$ — this requires either $a < 0$ (with appropriate $c$) or $a > 0$ with a phase shift

scoring_points.description: Correct height $H(1) = 35$ meters; supporting work must show substitution of $t = 1$ into the function
common_traps: At $t = 1$ the passenger is at the midline height (halfway up), not at the maximum — $\sin(\pi) = 0$, so $H = d = 35$ m

parts.prompt:
A passenger boards the Ferris wheel at the lowest point at $t = 0$ and rides for a full $12$ minutes (three complete revolutions).

(i) Find all values of $t$ in $0 < t < 12$ at which the passenger is at a height of exactly $35$ meters (the center height). Express your answers as exact values.

(ii) On the interval $0 < t < 1$, is the rate of change of $H$ positive or negative? Justify your answer using the graph of $H$.
parts.scoring_notes: For part (i), $H = 35$ means $\sin(\frac{\pi}{2}(t+1)) = 0$, giving $t + 1 = 2k$ so $t = 2k - 1$. In $(0, 12)$: $t = 1, 3, 5, 7, 9, 11$. All six values required. For part (ii), on $(0, 1)$ the passenger rises from minimum $5$ m toward midline $35$ m, so $H$ is increasing and the rate of change is positive. No calculus required — a reference to the increasing height is sufficient.

scoring_points.description: All six correct times $t = 1, 3, 5, 7, 9, 11$ in $(0, 12)$; no incorrect values
wrong_examples: Only $t = 1, 3, 5$ (missing the second and third revolution values)
Including $t = 0$ or $t = 12$ (not in the open interval $(0, 12)$)
common_traps: There are two midline crossings per revolution and three full revolutions in 12 minutes — six total midline crossings in $(0, 12)$

scoring_points.description: Correct answer 'positive' with justification that on $(0, 1)$ the passenger rises from minimum $5$ m to midline $35$ m, so $H$ is increasing

required_elements: passenger is rising (or $H$ is increasing) on $(0, 1)$
alternatives.correct_example: On $(0, 1)$, the passenger rises from the minimum height $5$ m toward the midline $35$ m, so $H$ is increasing on this interval. The rate of change of $H$ is therefore positive.

wrong_examples: 'Negative' — incorrect; the passenger starts at the lowest point and moves upward
'Positive because the Ferris wheel rotates' — not a mathematical justification
common_traps: The passenger boards at the minimum ($t = 0$, $H = 5$ m) and moves toward the midline at $t = 1$ ($H = 35$ m) — height is increasing, so rate of change is positive

parts.prompt:
(i) On the interval $0 < t < 2$, which of the following is true about $H$?
a. $H$ is positive and increasing.
b. $H$ is positive and decreasing.
c. $H$ is negative and increasing.
d. $H$ is negative and decreasing.

(ii) A second Ferris wheel at the same amusement park has a function $H_2(t) = 20\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{3}(t - 1.5)\right) + 40$ modeling passenger height, in meters. Find the first positive time $t$ at which both passengers are at the same height. Show the computations that lead to your answer, or indicate if no such $t$ exists on $0 < t \leq 6$.
parts.scoring_notes: For part (i), on $(0, 2)$ the passenger rises from $5$ m (minimum) through $35$ m (midline) to $65$ m (maximum) — heights are positive and $H$ is increasing throughout. Answer: (a). For part (ii), setting $H = H_2$ gives a transcendental equation requiring numerical/graphical solution. At $t = 0$: $H = 5$ and $H_2 = 20\sin(-\pi/2) + 40 = 20$; at $t = 1$: $H = 35$ and $H_2 = 20\sin(-\pi/6) + 40 = 30$. The crossing occurs near $t \approx 0.85$ (accept $[0.80, 0.90]$). Must show the equal-height setup.

scoring_points.description: Correct answer — choice (a): $H$ is positive and increasing on $(0, 2)$

alternatives.correct_example: (a) $H$ is positive and increasing on $(0, 2)$: the passenger rises from minimum $5$ m at $t=0$ through midline $35$ m at $t=1$ to maximum $65$ m at $t=2$ — always positive, always increasing.

wrong_examples: (b) positive and decreasing — incorrect, $H$ increases from min to max on $(0, 2)$
(c) or (d) negative — incorrect, minimum height is $5 > 0$
common_traps: If both a letter choice and a written description are given, the written description is scored — ensure the written description matches choice (a)

scoring_points.description: Correct setup $H(t) = H_2(t)$ and solution $t \approx 0.85$ (accept $[0.80, 0.90]$) obtained graphically or numerically

alternatives.correct_example: Setting $-30\sin(\frac{\pi}{2}(t+1)) + 35 = 20\sin(\frac{\pi}{3}(t-1.5)) + 40$; numerically/graphically, first positive intersection $\approx t \approx 0.85$ minutes.

wrong_examples: Solving for intersections without showing the equal-height equation
common_traps: An exact closed-form solution does not exist — numerical or graphical methods are required and expected; showing the equation setup earns partial credit even without a precise numerical answer
